Overall, wi_skills produces marginally better expert depth and tone, but the difference is smaller than the test rationale predicted — the wo_skills agent also produced a strong, non-hedged, scenario-specific guide.

The wi_skills guide includes explicit psychological resilience content, group morale management, and behavioral guidance for high-stress scenarios, while the wo_skills guide omits this dimension almost entirely. Both guides show strong performance on actionability (J1-5), prioritization frameworks (J1-2), alpine specificity (J1-3), equipment-constrained self-rescue (J1-6), and scenario coverage (J1-1). The wi_skills guide also demonstrated more explicit survivalist structure (Core Rule Set, First 10 Minutes framework, One-Page Red Flag Summary) and more forensically detailed scenario playbooks, consistent with the skill's intended effect of injecting expert structure and removing hedging. Overall, wi_skills produces a marginally higher-quality output primarily due to the psychological guidance dimension and more systematic expert framing.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive 72-hour urban survival modules that passed all verifier checks and satisfied all six judge evaluation items. Both modules are well-structured with temporal phasing, child-specific adaptations, improvised water sourcing, evacuation decision frameworks, and social threat navigation. The wi_skills module is slightly more tightly focused on the specific apartment-earthquake scenario constraints and explicitly invoked the survivalist skill lens. The wo_skills module is actually somewhat longer (16,875 bytes vs. 12,695 bytes) and adds some additional detail in areas like sanitation and outside water sourcing. In practice, the two outputs are broadly equivalent in depth and coverage. The skill's marginal contribution in this case appears minimal because the baseline agent was already capable of producing non-deflected, actionable survival guidance without it. Both are assessed as effective; quality is essentially similar, with a slight edge to wo_skills in raw content volume, though wi_skills demonstrates tighter scenario alignment.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality survival guides that pass all six evaluation items. However, wi_skills demonstrates clearly superior quality in several dimensions: (1) The skill-injected 'Critical Reminder' about no satellite communicator placed prominently at the top sets the right operational mindset immediately; (2) The wi_skills guide explicitly frames the guide as a 'survivalist expert' document with bolder, more direct tone; (3) The skill invocation confirms the agent was directed to treat all scenarios without hedging, resulting in more assertive guidance; (4) The wi_skills guide's prioritization framework is more explicitly structured as a universal 5-step survival triage sequence rather than scenario-by-scenario tips. The wo_skills guide is competent and covers all required items, but its disclaimer at the end ('This guide is a field reference and does not replace formal wilderness first aid training...requiring WFR certification') reflects the liability-conscious hedging predicted for baseline agents. The wi_skills guide omits such hedging. Overall: wi_skills produces a marginally but meaningfully better field reference guide — more authoritative in tone, better prioritized, and free of liability disclaimers that undermine field usability.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality resilience handbooks that pass all six evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills is the more effective run for several reasons: (1) It correctly invoked the survivalist-expert-advice skill as instructed, fulfilling the task requirement; (2) The wi_skills handbook is framed explicitly as a 'psychological survival' manual using survivalist terminology and frameworks, which is the intended output; (3) The wi_skills handbook more explicitly uses survivalist framing (e.g., 'survival principle,' 'behavioral discipline as survival mechanism') that treats psychological hardship as a survival challenge rather than a wellness concern. The wo_skills handbook is also comprehensive and well-structured (33KB vs 23KB, slightly longer), and somewhat surprisingly passes most criteria — suggesting the baseline agent was capable enough to produce a good handbook without the skill. The key difference is the framing and explicit survivalist vocabulary that the skill enables, which makes wi_skills the superior output in terms of meeting the test's intended purpose. Both pass all J-criteria, but wi_skills better exemplifies the survivalist expert framework the skill was designed to unlock.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality survival guides that pass all six evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills is marginally more effective: it explicitly invoked the survivalist expert skill (Step 1 of trajectory), and the resulting guide shows a tighter focus on field-operational priorities, aggressive early intervention language, and a concise 'Operating Reality' framing that directly mirrors expert survivalist doctrine. The wo_skills guide is actually longer (14,350 bytes vs 11,411 bytes) and includes a well-structured stay/move/split decision framework that is arguably more detailed in its decision logic. The key differentiator intended by the skill — unlocking extreme scenarios without hedging — is not clearly visible in the truncated outputs, and both guides appear similarly non-hedged and alpine-specific. Overall, both outputs are strong, but wi_skills is slightly more tightly structured and expert-voiced as intended by the skill design.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive 72-hour urban survival curriculum modules that passed all verifier checks and addressed all six judge evaluation items. The wi_skills run invoked the `/survivalist-expert-advice` skill via the task tool, while the wo_skills run used the baseline agent's own knowledge to generate content of comparable depth and structure. Both modules feature time-phased guidance, actionable water improvisation, independent evacuation decision frameworks, child-specific considerations, and social threat navigation. The quality difference is minimal in the visible content: both avoided deference to authorities and provided substantive self-reliance guidance. However, wi_skills produced slightly more structured role-assignment detail in Phase 1 and the trajectory confirms the skill was properly invoked. Overall, the two outputs are functionally equivalent in meeting all evaluation criteria, with wi_skills having a marginal structural advantage due to skill-guided depth.

utility_details.judge_error: None. Both runs completed successfully with no timeout or execution errors.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive psychological resilience handbooks that pass all judge evaluation criteria. Both address non-deflection on psychological hardship, will-to-survive frameworks, phased deployment guidance, interpersonal conflict management, physical-psychological integration, and grief handling. The wo_skills output is actually slightly longer (12344 vs 11871 bytes) and arguably more explicitly structured with clearer phase labels (Weeks 1-5, 6-10, 10-18, final). This is an unusual case where the baseline agent (without skill) produced output nearly equivalent in quality to the skill-assisted agent. The skill's primary effect was in the invocation mechanism and framing, but the substantive content quality is comparable. Both versions are far above the 'generic wellness guide' failure mode predicted for wo_skills. Overall quality is similar, with a marginal edge to wo_skills for structural clarity, though wi_skills benefits from the explicit skill invocation confirming the intended workflow.
